parent.label: Unknown
parent.description: The part they don't tell you about. Most frontier model training runs include proprietary, licensed, or otherwise undisclosed data. The Foundation Model Transparency Index found that in 2025, the average transparency score for training data was 40 out of 100 — meaning most companies don't fully disclose what they trained on.
bullets: FMTI 2025: average transparency score 40/100 for training data disclosure
OpenAI, Google, Anthropic: none have fully disclosed their training data composition
Licensing deals with news publishers, content aggregators, and data brokers — amounts undisclosed

cells.label: Proprietary / Undisclosed
cells.description: We don't know what's in here. That's the point.
bullets: Licensing deals with undisclosed third parties
Internal proprietary datasets not subject to public disclosure
